subject: Chemistry
solution:
Given the solubility of Ca3PO42 is Wg/100 mL. The solubility in mol/L is S=WM×1000100mol/L.

The equilibrium reaction of calcium phosphate is 

Ca3PO42⇌3Ca2++2PO43-At equilibrium3S2S

Now, Ksp=Ca2+3PO43-2

⇒Ksp=3S32S2=108×S5

⇒Ksp=108×WM×105⇒Ksp=108×105×WM5

=1.08×107WM5

subject: Chemistry
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \quad \mathrm{HX}_{(\mathrm{aq)}} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+}+\mathrm{X}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{-} \quad \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=4 \times 10^{-10} \\
& 0.01(1-\alpha) \quad 0.01 \alpha \quad 0.01 \alpha \quad \text { Not justified } \\
& \Rightarrow 0.01 \alpha=10^{-5} \Rightarrow \alpha=10^{-3} \\
& \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{a}}=0.01 \alpha^2=10^{-8}
\end{aligned}$
On dilution let final concentration of $\mathrm{HX}=\mathrm{c} \mathrm{M}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Hx}_{(\mathrm{an})} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+}+\mathrm{X}_{(\mathrm{an})}^{-} \\
& \mathrm{C}(1-\alpha) \quad \mathrm{C} \alpha \quad \mathrm{C} \alpha \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{C} \alpha=10^{-6}...(1) \\
& \quad \frac{\mathrm{C} \alpha^{2}}{1-\alpha}=\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=10^{-8}...(2) \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{10^{-6} \alpha}{1-\alpha}=10^{-8}
\end{aligned}$
Data given is inconsistent \& contradictory. This should be bonus.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Sodium acetate is a salt of strong base and weak acid.
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\therefore \mathrm{pH}=7+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{p} K_a+\frac{1}{2} \log c \quad \text { where } \\
&\mathrm{p} k_a=-\log K_a=-\log 10^{-5}=5 \\
&\log \mathrm{c}=\log 10^{-1}=-1 \\
&\mathrm{pH}=7+\frac{5}{2}-\frac{1}{2}=9.0
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Due to the common ion effect of chloride, the solubility of silver chloride is less in HCl and KCl, compared to solubility in water. In deionized water no common ion effect will take place so maximum solubility

AgCl(s)⇌Ag+(aq)+Cl-(aq)

subject: Chemistry
solution: Methyl orange is a weak acid. Additionally, in acidic conditions, methyl orange exists in the quinonoid form, which is red in color. In alkaline conditions, it exists in the benzenoid form, which is yellow in color. Since red is more deeply coloured than yellow, Statement-2 is wrong.

solution:
In pure water,

Ksp=S2=8×10-42

=64×10-8

In 0.01 M H2SO4

H2SO4(aq)x→2H(aq)+x+SO42-(aq.)(x+0.01)

Ksp=x(x+0.01)

=64×10-8

x+0.01≅0.01 M

So, x(0.01)=64×10-8

x=64×10-6M

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Given: KspPbI2=8×10-9

To calculate : solubility of PbI2 in 0.1M solution of PbNO22

I PbNO32→Pb(aq)+2+2NO3-aq

0.1 M---         0.1 M         0.2 M

II PbI2s⇌Pb+2aq+2I-aq

                         s                2s

[Pb+2]=s+0.1

≃0.1

Now: Ksp=8×10-9=Pb+2I-2

⇒8×10-9=0.1×2 s2

⇒8×10-8=4 s2⇒s=2×10-4

⇒S=141×10-6M

⇒x=141

subject: Chemistry
solution:
H2SO3 [Dibasic acid]

c=0.588 M

⇒ pH of solution is due to First dissociation only since Ka1>>Ka2

⇒ First dissociation of H2SO3

H2SO3(aq)⇌H⊕(aq)+HSO3-(aq) : ka1=1.7×10-2

t=      0

t       C-x              x                x

⇒Ka1=1.7100=H⊕HSO3-H2SO3

⇒1.7100=x20.588-x

⇒1.7×0.588-1.7x=100x2

⇒100x2+1.7x-1=0

⇒H⊕=x=-1.7+(1.7)2+4×100×12×100=0.09186

Therefore pH of sol. is :

pH=-logH⊕

⇒pH=-log0.09186=1.036≃1

subject: Chemistry
solution:
When ionic product i.e. the product of the concentration of ions in the solution exceeds the value of solubility product, formation of precitpiate occurs.
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{CaF}_2 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{~F}^{-} \\ & \text {Ionic product }=\left[\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}\right]\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]^2 \\ & \text { when, }\left[\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}\right]=1 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{M} \\ & {\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]^2=\left(1 \times 10^{-3}\right)^2 \mathrm{M} } \\ & =1 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{M} \\ \therefore & {\left[\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}\right]\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]^2=\left(1 \times 10^{-2}\right)\left(1 \times 10^{-6}\right)=1 \times 10^{-8} } \\ & \text { In this case, } \\ & \text { Ionic product }\left(1 \times 10^{-8}\right)> \\ & \text { solubility product }\left(1.7 \times 10^{-10}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Hence (a) is correct option.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
$\mathrm{Mg}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_2$
$\mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{sp}}=\left[\mathrm{Mg}^{2+}\right]\left[\mathrm{OH}^{-}\right]^2$
$\left[\mathrm{OH}^{-}\right]=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{sp}}}{\left[\mathrm{Mg}^{2+}\right]}}=10^{-4}$
$\therefore \mathrm{p}^{\mathrm{OH}}=4$ and $\mathrm{p}^{\mathrm{H}}=10$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Lewis base: Chemical species which has capability to donate electron pair.

In NF3, SF4, ClF3 central atom (i.e. N,S,Cl)
having lone pair therefore act as Lewis base.

In PCl5, central atom P does not have lone pair therefore does not act as Lewis base.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For mixture A:
Millimoles of OH$^{-}$ = $10 \times 0.2 \times 2 = 4$
Millimoles of H$^{+}$ = $25 \times 0.1 \times 1 = 2.5$
Remaining OH$^{-}$ = $4 - 2.5 = 1.5$ mmol
The solution is basic, so pH $> 7$.

For mixture B:
Millimoles of H$^{+}$ = $10 \times 0.01 \times 2 = 0.2$
Millimoles of OH$^{-}$ = $10 \times 0.01 \times 2 = 0.2$
The solution is completely neutralized, so pH $= 7$.

For mixture C:
Millimoles of H$^{+}$ = $10 \times 0.1 \times 2 = 2$
Millimoles of OH$^{-}$ = $10 \times 0.1 \times 1 = 1$
Remaining H$^{+}$ = $2 - 1 = 1$ mmol
The solution is acidic, so pH $< 7$.

Therefore, the increasing order of pH values is C $<$ B $<$ A.

Answer: $C < B < A$

$\Rightarrow \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=10^{-5}$

solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{pOH}=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{b}}+\log \frac{\left[\mathrm{NH}_4^{+}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{NH}_3\right]} \\
& \mathbf{p O H}=4.745
\end{aligned}$
on adding 0.05 mole HCl
$\begin{aligned}
& \begin{array}{llll}
\mathrm{NH}_3+ & \mathrm{H}^{\oplus} & \rightarrow & \mathrm{NH}_4^{\oplus} \\
0.1 & 0.05 & & 0.1 \\
0.05 & 0 & & 0.15
\end{array} \\
& \mathrm{pOH}^{\prime}=4.745+\log 3 \\
& \mathrm{pOH}^{\prime}-\mathrm{pOH}=0.477 \\
& 14-\mathrm{pH}^{\prime}-14+\mathrm{pH}=0.477 \\
& \Delta \mathrm{pH}=0.477 \\
& =47.7 \times 10^{-2} \approx 48 \times 10^{-2}
\end{aligned}$
